medications.drug_name: no new medication to be self-administered
medications.indication_rationale: There is no medication the telehealth AI Doctor should direct the patient to take at home for suspected symptomatic AAA. Analgesia, fluid resuscitation, and any peri-operative medication decisions belong to the emergency/vascular surgical team once the patient is in hospital.

Self-administered NSAIDs should not be recommended in suspected AAA — they do not address the underlying vascular emergency, may mask symptom severity, and could complicate later surgical/anaesthetic management.
contraindications_in_this_case: Suspected surgical emergency — analgesia decisions belong to the treating emergency team

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Remain lying down/supine and avoid any exertion, straining, or standing unnecessarily while awaiting ambulance transfer.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Any increase in intra-abdominal pressure or blood pressure (straining, exertion) raises aortic wall stress and the risk of rupture in an already unstable, expanding aneurysm.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Practice slow, controlled breathing while waiting for the ambulance to help keep heart rate and blood pressure from spiking.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reducing acute anxiety-driven tachycardia and blood pressure spikes may reduce additional aortic wall shear stress while awaiting transfer.

patient_education_points.point: The strong pulsing feeling in your belly, together with the worsening pain in your stomach and back and the lightheadedness, could mean the main blood vessel in your abdomen is dangerously stretched and at risk of a serious bleed. This needs to be checked in hospital right now, not tomorrow.
Please call 000 for an ambulance now rather than driving yourself or waiting to see a GP — if this worsens suddenly, you need paramedics with you, not to be behind the wheel of a car.
While you wait for the ambulance: lie down flat, stay as still and calm as possible, and don't eat or drink anything in case you need surgery.
Unlock your front door if you can do so safely, or ask your spouse to, so paramedics can get in quickly.

follow_up_plan.interval: Superseded by emergency escalation — follow-up arranged by ED and vascular surgery after admission
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Post-discharge: GP review for blood pressure control, smoking cessation if relevant, and ongoing vascular surveillance per vascular surgery advice
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: If pain suddenly becomes severe/tearing, if the patient faints or loses consciousness, or profound sweating/pallor develops — call 000 immediately, do not wait.

must_include_items: Call 000 for an ambulance immediately — do not self-transport
Explicit statement that the pulsatile sensation and pain pattern may represent a serious blood vessel problem (aneurysm) that needs urgent hospital assessment
Instruction to lie down/remain still, avoid exertion, and not eat or drink pending transfer
Safety-netting for sudden severe pain, collapse, or profound sweating while awaiting ambulance
acceptable_alternatives: 'Suspected AAA', 'possible aneurysm', 'serious blood vessel problem in your abdomen' are all acceptable framings of the diagnosis statement
errors_of_omission: No emergency escalation recommended — patient not directed to call 000 or go to ED
No mention that this could be a vascular/aneurysm emergency
No instruction to remain still/supine and avoid exertion
errors_of_commission: Recommending the patient wait until morning or book a routine GP appointment
Recommending self-transport by car instead of ambulance
Attributing symptoms to musculoskeletal strain or indigestion without escalation
Recommending NSAIDs or other self-administered analgesia as the primary management action
Requiring physical examination confirmation (palpation/bruit) before agreeing to escalate
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: CRITICAL CASE: Any response that does not include both (1) emergency ambulance call (000) AND (2) explicit framing that this may be a serious vascular/aneurysm emergency is an automatic FAIL on the management domain. This is a T5 case — the minimum acceptable output is emergency escalation.
